question.text: A Professional Clearing Member (PCM) is entitled to:

answer.text: Clear and settle trades executed by other members but NOT execute any trades on the exchange
Both trade and clear on their own account
Trade only on their own account
Only provide research and advisory services

explanation.text: **The Logic:** PCMs can clear and settle trades of TMs/TCMs and their clients but CANNOT execute trades. Typically banks and custodians become PCMs. **The Trap:** PCMs are clearing-only members. They don't trade. They don't provide advisory services. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VII covers clearing member roles. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Banks often become PCMs to offer clearing services to their brokerage clients — leveraging their capital adequacy and settlement infrastructure.
